codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the main layout/theme scaffolding (header/footer/mobile nav/theme toggle/semantic structure), but a blocking requirement is missed: page transition animation does not implement reduced-motion handling.

issues.description: Route transition always animates (`initial`/`animate` with duration 0.3) and has no `prefers-reduced-motion` gate. Because `src/app/template.tsx` wraps all page content in `PageTransition` (line 4), users with reduced-motion preference still get non-essential page transition animation, violating expected behavior "Reduced-motion respected" (VAL-FOUND-020).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill mandates TDD with failing tests first, but the transcript for this session shows implementation files were created before `src/__tests__/layout.test.ts` and no failing-test phase was captured for new layout tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton order includes `Create .../src/components/layout/header.tsx` and other layout component files before `Create .../src/__tests__/layout.test.ts`; `Execute npx vitest run --reporter=verbose` appears before and after implementation rather than a failing run immediately after adding new tests.

summary: Review failed due to missing reduced-motion handling in the page transition implementation. Core layout functionality is present, but this gap leaves a required accessibility behavior unmet.
